title: Microsoft blocks registry trick that unlocked performance-boosting native NVMe driver on Windows 11 workarounds still exist to enable support, however
summary: Microsoft blocked a registry trick to enable native NVMe driver on Windows 11. However, third-party tools can still help users bypass this limitation. This workaround allows for improved NVMe performance.
insight: Native NVMe driver enables improved storage performance on Windows 11 PCs

title: Nvidia CEO says he's 'empathetic' to DLSS 5 concerns Jensen Huang doubles down on defense while decrying 'AI slop'
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang shows empathy for concerns about DLSS 5's 'AI slop'. He defends DLSS 5 while criticizing what he considers 'AI slop' in other AI-generated content. This tone shift aims to address concerns and maintain trust with customers.
insight: Nvidia CEO addresses concerns about AI-generated content quality and accuracy

title: AMD's Enterprise CPU and GPU roadmap: Venice, Verano, Zen 6, Helios, and CDNA
summary: AMD has a robust roadmap for 2026 and 2027, featuring new CPUs and GPUs. The Venice and Verano processors, Zen 6, and Helios platform are set to enhance data center performance. This includes AI-focused GPU accelerators like the Instinct MI500.
insight: Advanced data center capabilities drive AMD's competitive market edge

title: GTC 2026: Ian Buck press Q&A transcript VP of Hyperscale and HPC speaks out on shelving CPX and shipping LPU decode this year
summary: Ian Buck discussed Nvidia's shift in strategy, shelving CPX and focusing on LPU, during a post-GTC 2026 press Q&A. The VP of Hyperscale and HPC addressed Nvidia's recent acquisition of Groq and its implications on the company's product roadmap. Buck provided insights into Nvidia's future plans and priorities.
insight: Nvidia prioritizes LPU over CPX, marking a significant product shift.

title: LG produces the world's first mass-production LCD laptop display capable of 1 Hz to save power OLED version arriving in 2027
summary: LG introduces a groundbreaking LCD laptop display that can reduce its refresh rate to 1 Hz to conserve power, mirroring high-end smartphone LTPO displays. This innovative technology aims to provide longer battery life for laptops. Mass production is expected to begin with an OLED version following in 2027.
insight: Prolonged battery life boosts laptop usability and user satisfaction.

title: YouTuber upgrades 14-year-old ThinkPad laptop with a $25 DIY IPS display simple panel swap breathes new life into 2012-era machine with 'visually noticeable' results
summary: A YouTuber upgraded a 2012 Lenovo ThinkPad X230 with a $25 IPS display, yielding visually noticeable results.
insight: Budget-friendly display swaps can breathe new life into old laptops.

title: Imagination Tech working on mainstream PC gaming with ambitious graphics card and SoC design companies shows off progress with DirectX 11 workloads
summary: Imagination Technologies is working on a high-performance graphics card and SoC design for mainstream PC gaming, aiming to bring PowerVR graphics to desktop, workstation, and cloud environments.
insight: Competition for AMD and NVIDIA in the graphics processing market.

title: Apple MacBook Air (13-inch, M5) review: Steady as it goes
summary: The MacBook Air with M5 delivers strong performance and premium materials, making it a valuable computing option.

title: Crimson Desert devs apologize for confusion over Intel GPU FAQ backtracks over prior dismissive language regarding Arc graphics support

title: The legendary AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D just hit its lowest-ever price on Amazon grab our favorite gaming CPU with a $60 saving for undeniably brilliant performance
summary: The AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D is now available at an all-time low price of $419.95, making it a highly sought-after CPU option for gamers seeking superior performance.
insight: Game-changing performance at a discounted price point for gamers.

title: Intel Core Ultra 7 270K Plus review: Back from the brink
summary: The Intel Core Ultra 7 270K Plus offers exceptional application performance, but struggles with gaming. Despite improvements from Intel's Arrow Lake Refresh and a new Binary Optimization Tool, gaming performance remains a concern. This processor shows promise but still has limitations.
insight: Application performance outshines gaming capabilities for this Intel processor.

title: Get the 9800X3D CPU, Asus TUF gaming X870, and 32GB of DDR5 RAM, plus a free 512GB Gen 4 SSD and Crimson Desert, all for $959.99 Newegg's combo bundles strike again

title: Concerns raised over Shahed kamikaze drone listings on Alibaba they featured AI guidance to lock onto people, building, vehicles, ships, etc
summary: Alibaba removed listings and suspended sellers advertising kamikaze-style drones with AI guidance, sparking concerns.
insight: Sale of AI-equipped kamikaze drones raises national security and ethics concerns.

title: Valve adds early Steam Machine support in SteamOS 3.8 latest update brings performance gains, better controller support, and desktop improvements
summary: SteamOS 3.8.0 preview introduces early Steam Machine support, performance upgrades, and improved VRR.

title: Intel's long-rumored Core Ultra 3 205 CPU finally appears for sale at French retailer priced at $182, early listing confirms previously-known specs for the chip
summary: The Intel Core Ultra 3 205 is an 8-core CPU with 4 performance and 4 efficiency cores, part of the Arrow Lake family. It's been spotted at a French retailer despite never officially launching. The chip's specs and availability for order confirm previous rumors.
insight: Long-rumored CPU finally surfaces, revealing Intel's unannounced product strategy.

title: Jensen Huang says Nvidia engineers should use AI tokens worth half their annual salary every year to be fully productive compares not using AI to using paper and pencil for designing chips
summary: Nvidia's CEO Jensen Huang urges engineers to use AI tokens worth half their salary to boost productivity.
insight: AI adoption can 10x engineer productivity and transform design processes.

title: UV resin injection cures ancient, cataract-inflicted CRT monitor repaired 20-inch Trinitron would have been an enviable display in the mid 1990s
summary: A vintage 20-inch Trinitron CRT was repaired using UV resin injection, curing cataracts that affected its display. This process allowed the monitor to regain its original image quality. The repair is a testament to creative problem-solving.
insight: Reviving vintage electronics highlights ingenuity in repurposing obsolete technology.

title: MacBook Neo costs more in Portugal due to copyright levy for piracy compensation taxes storage costs extra in multiple countries, thanks to draconian laws that pre-punish buyers
summary: MacBook Neo prices are higher in Portugal due to a copyright levy for piracy compensation. This fee is a result of draconian laws that pre-punish buyers before any infringement occurs. The extra cost is a tax on storage devices in multiple countries.
insight: Draconian piracy laws unfairly burden consumers with pre-emptive fines.

title: Mad lad stores and loads Doom from within DNS TXT record type abused to store game data
summary: A mad individual exploited DNS TXT records to store Doom game data, allowing for the game to be loaded from within a DNS record. This was achieved by abusing the DNS system's flexibility and the fact that TXT records can hold arbitrary text data. The hack demonstrates a creative yet unconventional use of technology.
insight: Abusing DNS TXT records exposes critical infrastructure vulnerabilities and security risks.

title: RTX 5060 in a house fire suffers melted shroud and fans, but survives with PCB intact scorched GPU just needed a cleanup and a new cooler for full restoration
summary: An RTX 4060 survived a house fire with minimal damage. The GPU's PCB remained intact despite flames, requiring a new cooler for full restoration. A thorough cleaning was necessary to revive the scorched component.
insight: High-quality components can withstand extreme temperatures with proper design.

title: Hugging Face s Reachy Mini is an open-source AI robot for your computer or Raspberry Pi CM4
summary: Hugging Face's Reachy Mini is an open-source AI robot that can interface with the physical world, featuring a camera, microphones, and servo motors. It's available in two versions: the Reachy Mini Lite for computers and the Reachy Mini Wireless autonomous robot for Raspberry Pi CM4. The robot's SDK allows for easy app development and integration with LLMs.
insight: AI robots enable new interfaces between code and the physical world.

title: ESP-IDF v6.0 framework adds support for ESP32-C5 and ESP32-C61, preview for ESP32-H21 and ESP32-H4
summary: Espressif Systems released ESP-IDF v6.0 with support for ESP32-C5 and ESP32-C61, and preview support for ESP32-H21 and ESP32-H4. The framework includes a new Installation Manager, Picolibc C library, and security updates. It also enables Wi-Fi enhancements and over-the-air bootloader updates.
insight: Enhanced IoT development framework for resource-constrained wireless microcontrollers

title: Ampisu is a compact pocket-sized USB lab power supply with SCPI and web control (Crowdfunding)
summary: The Ampisu is a compact, pocket-sized lab power supply with SCPI and web control, designed for low-power embedded work and field debugging. It features a Raspberry Pi RP2040 dual-core microcontroller, three isolated output channels, and non-volatile memory for saving configurations. The power supply adapts to various input voltages via USB Type-C port.
insight: First pocket-sized lab power supply with web control capabilities

title: More Rising Stars Sixth Group
summary: Fierce Sensors introduces its 2026 class of Rising Stars at Sensors Converge, May 5-7, in Santa Clara, CA. This prestigious group will be honored for their contributions to the field of sensors. Their work has the potential to drive innovation and advancement.
insight: Accelerating innovation through recognition of emerging sensor technology leaders.

title: Why the Majority of AI-Powered IoT Projects Fail: Jack Gold
summary: 75% of AIoT pilot projects fail to reach production due to various issues. This highlights the complexity and challenges of integrating AI into IoT systems. Effective deployment requires careful planning and execution.
insight: Integration challenges hinder AIoT project success and adoption.

title: Meet this smart building cowboy from Tulsa
summary: Meet Mango, a smart building expert from Radix IoT in Tulsa, specializing in transforming older CityPlex towers into efficient and technologically advanced giants through innovative management and IoT solutions.

title: The memory crunch monster is real for edge devs
summary: Memory shortages drive up prices, affecting edge projects' sensor-laden budgets. Edge developers face increased costs and project delays due to rising memory prices. This trend is expected to worsen as shortages persist.

title: Berkeley births intelligent spectral sensor for industry use
summary: The University of Berkeley has developed a spectral sensor that can detect target objects. This sensor significantly increases the speed of digital processing, up to 100 times. It is designed for industry use.

title: "We've achieved AGI," says Nvidia CEO, but his own examples suggest otherwise

The tension between those remarks one heralding the dawn of... Read Entire Article

title: How BYD engineered breakthrough five-minute EV charging
summary: BYD developed Flash Chargers capable of delivering up to 1,500 kW, allowing for rapid EV charging. In tests, select BYD batteries charged from 10% to 70% in about five minutes and from 10% to 97% in around 12 minutes. This technology aims to greatly reduce EV charging times.
insight: Rapid charging reduces range anxiety and boosts EV adoption.

title: Operation Alice: The dark web isn't as hidden as it seems, as global crackdown shows
summary: Europol's Operation Alice dismantled a large dark web network with fraudulent websites, with a 2-year investigation focusing on a platform named Alice with Violence CP. The operation ultimately took down one of the largest dark web networks. The investigation began in 2021.
insight: Dark web isn't as hidden as it seems with global law enforcement efforts.

title: Windows 11 users are still fixing the Start menu with third-party tools
summary: Windows 11 users rely on third-party tools to customize the Start menu, with options ranging from free, customizable interfaces to polished modern tweaks. Open Shell, Start11, StartAllBack, and ExplorerPatcher are popular choices among users. These tools offer a way to take back control of the UI.
insight: Users prioritize customization over Windows 11's default Start menu design.

title: Sony confirms AI frame generation is coming to PlayStation, just not this year
summary: Sony is developing AI-powered frame generation for the PlayStation, but it's not coming this year.
insight: Enhanced gaming performance through AI-powered frame generation technology arrives
